description:
A Solar Energy Systems Engineer at a utility-scale developer is evaluating a 10 MW ground-mount project to determine if a 4% price premium for bifacial modules is justified. Quantify the annual energy gain from bifacial technology over high-albedo desert terrain using PySAM's PVWattsv8 module and the weather file path provided at /home/ga/.SAM/weather_file_path.txt. Model a 10 MW (DC) utility-scale PV system with 1-axis tracking (Array type 2, DC:AC ratio 1.2, 0° tilt, 180° azimuth, 0.35 GCR, 14% losses). Compare a monofacial configuration (bifaciality 0, monthly albedo 0.20) against a bifacial configuration (bifaciality 0.70, monthly albedo 0.30). Calculate the bifacial gain percentage and export the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/bifacial_comparison.json using the following JSON structure:

{
  "system_capacity_kw": 10000,
  "dc_ac_ratio": 1.2,
  "array_type": 2,
  "gcr": 0.35,
  "losses_percent": 14,
  "bifaciality": 0.70,
  "monofacial_albedo": 0.20,
  "bifacial_albedo": 0.30,
  "monofacial_annual_energy_kwh": <computed>,
  "bifacial_annual_energy_kwh": <computed>,
  "bifacial_gain_percent": <computed>
}
